title: Can't pull public image from ghcr.io: 403
body:
### Issue Description

When trying to pull any public image from ghcr.io I get 403 status code from the server:
```sh
❯ podman logout --all
Removed login credentials for all registries
❯ podman pull ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest
Trying to pull ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest...
Error: initializing source docker://ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest:
Requesting bearer token: invalid status code from registry 403 (Forbidden)
```

Note that immediately after logging in using a personal access token pulling works:
```sh
❯ podman login ghcr.io
Authenticating with existing credentials for ghcr.io
Existing credentials are invalid, please enter valid username and password
Username (superfluffy): superfluffy
Password:
Login Succeeded!

❯ podman pull ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest
Trying to pull ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 63ab8c9a809b done
Copying blob 3689b8de819b done
Copying config e9c1a75695 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
e9c1a756955ae6924e299298961b2530505bc4d2d8267427af81a8d88e0f6a3f
```

Docker has no issues pulling images:
```sh
❯ docker logout ghcr.io
Removing login credentials for ghcr.io

❯ docker pull ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest
latest: Pulling from tomwright/dasel
3689b8de819b: Pull complete
63ab8c9a809b: Pull complete
Digest: sha256:670b7e2e6ebc711eb3e9ea4a69d7e5b835253bbac2ab3b8234f1f5bbb4a93a58
Status: Downloaded newer image for ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest
ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman logout --all`
2. `podman pull ghcr.io/tomwright/dasel:latest`
3. observe error


### Describe the results you received

I received an error with a 403 error status and message `Requesting bearer token: invalid status code from registry 403 (Forbidden)`

### Describe the results you expected

I expected podman to successfully pull the public image similar to docker or when I log into my github account.

### podman info output

```yaml
❯ podman info
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.29.0
  cgroupControllers:

      size: 65536

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Bare metal:
```
❯ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.4
API Version:  4.4.4

❯ pacman -Qi podman

Version         : 4.4.4-1

Architecture    : x86_64

Licenses        : Apache
Groups          : None
Provides        : None
Depends On      : catatonit  conmon  containers-common  crun  gcc-libs  glibc  iptables
                  device-mapper  libdevmapper.so=1.02-64  gpgme  libgpgme.so=11-64
                  libseccomp  libseccomp.so=2-64  slirp4netns

                  btrfs-progs: support btrfs backend devices [installed]
                  cni-plugins: for an alternative container-network-stack implementation
                  [installed]
                  fuse-overlayfs: for storage driver in rootless environment [installed]
                  podman-compose: for docker-compose compatibility [installed]
                  podman-docker: for Docker-compatible CLI
Required By     : podman-compose
Optional For    : None
Conflicts With  : None
Replaces        : None
Installed Size  : 79.24 MiB
Packager        : David Runge <dvzrv@archlinux.org>
Build Date      : Tue Mar 28 15:21:36 2023
Install Date    : Fri Mar 31 22:40:27 2023
Install Reason  : Explicitly installed
Install Script  : No
Validated By    : Signature

❯ uname -srvmpio
Linux 6.2.8-arch1-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Wed, 22 Mar 2023 22:52:35 +0000 x86_64 unknown unknown GNU/Linux
```

Archlinux builds podman from the v4.4.4 tagged ref:
https://github.com/archlinux/svntogit-community/blob/18ef17c01ae18f9e8faaf8d33ef8afe2d29ab529/trunk/PKGBUILD

### Additional information

_No response_

@vrothberg 

It looks like it worked. I apologize for the noise, but I don't understand why it works out of a sudden.

@SuperFluffy, no worries at all.  I appreciate you taking the time to reach out.

It could very well be a bug on the registry side or some maintenance window etc.  I suggest to close the issue for now but please reach out again if you see it popping up again.
